meaning: adj.风度雍容如贵妇的,温雅的
examples: Her language was not very ladylike.
She crossed the room with quick, ladylike steps.
I hate to be blunt, Frankie, but she just didn't strike me as being very ladylike.
For every bouncy miniskirt there was a ladylike pencil, and broad-shouldered military coats were offset by gently molded oversized ones.
I've now met her twice in the last few months, and I'd go so far as to describe her as genteel, even ladylike.
